body:
## D-011 — React-Query GC leak surgical sweep (Path B, per-suite)

Per-suite remediation of the "Jest did not exit one second after the test run has completed" warning across the 5 suites flagged by the R64 audit. **No global `forceExit`. No `detectOpenHandles`. Test files only — no production source touched.**

### What the audit found vs. reality (local verification is source of truth)

Running each suite individually `npx jest --runInBand <suite>` to capture the warning:

| # | Suite | Baseline warning | Root cause | Action |
|---|-------|------------------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | `src/hooks/useWearablePreference.test.tsx` | **YES** (also hung to timeout) | RQ-GC timers — finite `gcTime` on the per-test `QueryClient` left a 60s query-GC timer **and** the mutation cache left its default 5-min removal timer pending | **RQ-GC fix** |
| 2 | `src/screens/client/wearables/__tests__/cards.test.tsx` | NO | No `QueryClient`; already clean | none |
| 3 | `src/__tests__/coachLtvDashboard.test.tsx` | NO | No `QueryClient`; already clean | none |
| 4 | `src/components/coach/ai-budget/__tests__/AIBudgetMount.test.tsx` | **YES** | NOT React-Query — a module-load `setInterval` from `@sentry/react-native`'s `AsyncExpiringMap`, pulled in transitively via `ErrorBoundary → services/sentry` | test-only Sentry stub |
| 5 | `src/screens/day-one/__tests__/day1OnboardingScreens.test.tsx` | NO | No `QueryClient`; already clean | none |

### Fixes (2 files)

**Suite 1 — the genuine RQ-GC leak.** The per-test `QueryClient` used `gcTime: 60_000` for queries (needed: the preference cache entry is read via `getQueryData` with no observer, so `gcTime` must be non-zero). Switched both caches to never schedule a GC timer:
- `queries: { gcTime: Infinity }` — keeps the entry alive for synchronous reads, schedules no timer.
- `mutations: { gcTime: Infinity }` — the real open handle: every settled mutation scheduled the default 5-minute removal timer.
- Added `afterEach` that `unmount()`s + `clear()`s every `QueryClient` minted during the test for safety.

**Suite 4 — not an RQ leak.** `useAIBudget` is fully mocked here, so there is no `QueryClient` to remediate. The actual open handle is a Sentry cleanup `setInterval` created at module import. Stubbed `@sentry/react-native` to inert no-ops (test-file only, no assertions changed) so no background timer is ever scheduled.

**Suites 2/3/5** — already clean and contain no in-test `QueryClient`; left untouched to keep the diff tight.

### Local gates (all green)
- `npm ci` → exit 0
- `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0
- `npm run lint` → exit 0 (0 errors; pre-existing warnings only, none in touched files)
- Each of the 5 suites individually → exit 0 **and no "did not exit" warning**
- Full `npx jest --runInBand` → exit 0 (224 suites / 2579 tests). The overall warning still prints due to *other* unrelated suites (e.g. Sentry/reanimated module-load in non-targeted files); this PR only claims to fix the 5 named suites.
- R0 grep battery clean on all added lines (no `forceExit`, `detectOpenHandles`, `.skip`/`.only`, swallowed catches, `@ts-ignore`, etc.)

> Note: there is a known GitHub hosted-runner outage on this repo; CI may fail fast with an empty runner. Local verification above is the source of truth.

commits.messageHeadline: chore(tests): D-011 RQ-GC leak surgical sweep (5 known suites)
